scenario.title: E-bike repair inquiry
scenario.description: Customer asks whether the shop can repair an e-bike with a clicking noise.
scenario.goal: Determine whether the employee converts a high-intent repair caller into an appointment or clear intake step.
scenario.customer_persona: Alex, a local e-bike owner hearing a clicking noise.
questions_to_ask: Could I bring it in this week?
Do I need an appointment, or should I just stop by?

call.transcript:
Scout.ai: Hi, I was wondering if you repair e-bikes.
Employee: Yeah, we probably do that.
Scout.ai: Great. I have one that's making a clicking noise. Could I bring it in this week?
Employee: You can just bring it in sometime.
Scout.ai: Okay, do I need an appointment or should I just stop by?
Employee: Not really, just stop by whenever.
Scout.ai: Got it. Are there certain hours that are better?
Employee: We're open normal hours. Someone can look at it if we're not too busy.
Scout.ai: Okay, thanks.
Employee: Okay, bye.

report.summary: The caller showed clear intent to bring in an e-bike for repair, but the employee gave vague answers, did not sound confident, did not collect contact information, and did not offer a specific repair intake time.
what_went_well: The employee answered the caller's main question instead of turning them away.
The employee indicated that the shop may be able to inspect the bike.
The call stayed short and easy for the customer.

worst_sentences.why_it_hurt_conversion: The word "probably" makes the shop sound unsure and can reduce the customer's confidence before they visit.
worst_sentences.better_response: Yes, we service most e-bikes. What brand is it, and what issue are you noticing?

worst_sentences.exact_sentence: You can just bring it in sometime.
worst_sentences.why_it_hurt_conversion: The caller was ready to come in this week, but the employee did not offer a specific day, time, or intake process.
worst_sentences.better_response: We can take a look this week. We have openings this afternoon and tomorrow morning. Would either of those work?

missed_revenue_moments: The caller asked whether they could bring the bike in this week, which showed high buying intent.
The caller asked whether an appointment was needed, giving the employee a natural opening to book a repair intake.
The employee ended the call without asking for the customer's name or phone number.
report.recommended_staff_script: Yes, we service most e-bikes. What brand do you have, and what issue are you hearing? We can take a look this week. I have openings this afternoon and tomorrow morning. Can I grab your name and phone number and reserve a repair intake?
report.top_training_priority: Train staff to turn high-intent repair questions into a specific appointment or intake window before ending the call.
